choices.text: IL2CPP. Apple requires AOT compilation; Mono uses JIT (disallowed on iOS for security). IL2CPP compiles C# → C++ at build time → Xcode compiles to native. Recommended for all production builds (faster runtime + smaller stripped code). Android requires for 64-bit (Play Store requirement). Trade-off: longer build times; faster runtime.

questions.explanation: IL2CPP. Apple requires AOT. Recommended for all production builds. Android 64-bit requires it. Mono only for dev on platforms that allow.

questions.prompt: Scene with 500 trees causes performance drop due to 5000+ draw calls. Fix?

choices.text: GPU Instancing + Static Batching + LOD Groups. GPU Instancing on materials renders many copies as one draw call. Static Batching combines static meshes. LOD Groups render distant trees as low-poly. Combined: 5000 → ~10-50 draw calls. Foliage tool for grass / dense vegetation. Profile via Frame Debugger.

questions.explanation: GPU Instancing + Static Batching + LOD Groups. Combined: 5000 → 10-50 draw calls. Plus occlusion / frustum culling. Foliage tool for grass.

questions.prompt: Save large structured player data where?

choices.text: JSON file in Application.persistentDataPath. JsonUtility serializes complex structures; File.WriteAllText to per-user save location. PlayerPrefs only for small settings (high scores, audio volumes). For full save games: JSON + persistentDataPath + multiple save slots + versioning. Cross-platform (Unity manages OS-specific paths). Steam Cloud auto-syncs persistentDataPath on PC.

questions.explanation: JSON to Application.persistentDataPath. JsonUtility for serialization. Multiple slots + versioning. PlayerPrefs only for small settings.

questions.prompt: Game ships; players report random crashes. Identify root causes?

choices.text: Set up crash reporting (Unity Cloud Diagnostics, Firebase Crashlytics, or Sentry). SDK auto-captures stack traces + device info + breadcrumbs; uploads to web dashboard; aggregated crash groups; fix by priority. Non-negotiable for shipped games. Setup: 30-60 min; lifetime value: enormous. Combined with analytics for player behavior insight.

questions.explanation: Crash reporting (Unity Cloud Diagnostics / Firebase Crashlytics / Sentry). Auto-captures crashes; dashboard shows aggregated groups + stack traces; fix systematically. Non-negotiable for shipped games.

choices.text: itch.io. No fees, no review, instant. WebGL or PC build → upload → live within minutes. Limited audience reach but ideal for first projects + iteration + portfolio. Higher-audience platforms (Steam, Google Play) have more barriers but bigger reach. Console = highest prestige + barrier (dev program, certification, devkits).

questions.explanation: itch.io. No fees, no review, instant distribution. Ideal for first projects. Steam for broader audience after quality. Console = highest barrier.
